categories.title: WHO WROTE THAT?
categories.theme: Literary works matched to their authors — answer is the AUTHOR'S NAME

clues.clue: This British author gave us Pip, Miss Havisham, and the ghost of Jacob Marley across beloved Victorian novels.
Born Adeline Virginia Stephen, this modernist writer published groundbreaking novels under a married surname.
This Spanish author created the knight-errant Don Quixote, widely considered the first modern novel.
This French author penned the sweeping multi-volume work 'In Search of Lost Time', one of the longest novels ever written.
The short story collection 'Evenings on a Farm Near Dikanka' was an early work by this Russian author also known for 'The Overcoat'.

categories.title: WHO WROTE THAT?
categories.theme: Famous literary works and their authors — answer is the AUTHOR

clues.clue: This British author gave the world the boy wizard Harry Potter and his friends at Hogwarts.
This Victorian author penned the ghost story 'A Christmas Carol', featuring the miserly Ebenezer Scrooge.
This Colombian Nobel laureate wrote a sweeping romance set against a backdrop of disease, exploring Florentino Ariza's decades-long devotion.
This American authored 'The Legend of Sleepy Hollow', introducing readers to the terrifying Headless Horseman of a Hudson Valley town.
This Irish-born author, best known for modernist fiction, penned the semi-autobiographical 'A Portrait of the Artist as a Young Man' in 1916.

finals.title: DYSTOPIAN DETAILS
finals.clue: In this Aldous Huxley novel set in a World State, the words 'Mother' and 'Father' are considered obscene, and citizens are conditioned from birth to accept their place in a rigid caste system.

finals.title: LITERARY HEROINES
finals.clue: In Ayn Rand's 1957 novel, this character — whose name is also a question posed throughout the book — persuades the world's leading industrialists to abandon their companies and withdraw from society.
